commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2af0315423024cff5f6dc5b0b4ed2489d70f2e13)
commands.review_summary:
The branch sets Discord inbound context building to preserve bot-authored reply-target bodies while continuing to suppress referenced media, and updates shared and Discord regression tests.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +36. Total +37 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main marks Discord bot-authored reply targets as `isSelf`, the shared finalizer defaults `suppressSelfQuoteBody` to true, and the Discord regression test expects `ReplyToBody` to stay undefined. I did not run a live Discord gateway replay.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer needs to decide whether Discord may reintroduce bot-authored reply bodies broadly or define a narrower opt-in before any repair is safe.

target.title: [codex] Preserve Discord self-reply context
target.body:
## Summary

- preserve Discord reply-target text when a user replies to a bot-authored message
- keep self-quote media suppressed so replying to prior bot output does not refetch or reattach bot media
- add shared inbound-context and Discord process regression coverage

## Root cause

Discord reply context was resolved with a quoted message body, but the shared channel inbound finalizer suppresses self-authored quote bodies by default. Discord used that default even for explicit user replies to bot-authored messages, so the agent received `reply_to_id` metadata without `ReplyToBody`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A Discord user can reply to an OpenClaw/SnabbelClaw alert or prior bot message and the agent receives the replied-to message body as reply context.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, with dependencies installed by `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, running the patched Discord inbound context builder through `node --import tsx`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran this source command after the patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --eval 'import { createBaseDiscordMessageContext } from "./extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.test-harness.ts"; import { buildDiscordMessageProcessContext } from "./extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.context.ts"; /* build a Discord reply to a bot-authored alert, then print ctxPayload reply/media fields */'
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the patched source command:

```json
{
  "ReplyToId": "m-bot-alert",
  "ReplyToSender": "SnabbelClaw",
  "ReplyToBody": "Cron job \\\"WhatsApp Incremental Archive\\\" failed 1 times\\nLast error: check git status (agent) failed",
  "MediaPath": null,
  "MediaPaths": null
}
```

Observed result after fix: The runtime Discord inbound context contains the bot-authored alert text in `ReplyToBody`, while `MediaPath` and `MediaPaths` stay null for the self-quote attachment.

What was not tested: A live Discord gateway session against the public Discord API was not run.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.context.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- independent Codex subagent review: no findings
target.author: haukeschnau
